assertion_results.text: The plan explicitly references reusing common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter for comma-separated multi-value query parameter parsing rather than writing new parsing logic (constraint 5.4)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md states: 'Call apply_filter from common/src/db/query.rs to handle the license value. apply_filter already handles: Comma-separated multi-value parsing, SQL IN clause generation, Single-value exact-match filtering.' Also: 'No manual parsing of comma-separated values is performed here — that responsibility belongs to apply_filter in the service layer.'

assertion_results.text: The plan references following the filter pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs as a structural guide for implementing the license filter

assertion_results.text: The plan references using entity/src/package_license.rs for the JOIN query rather than writing raw SQL or creating a new entity
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md states: 'Join through the package_license entity...Use SeaORM's relation-based join (e.g., .join() or .find_also_related()) referencing the PackageLicense entity — do NOT write raw SQL or create a new entity.'

assertion_results.text: The reuse analysis covers all 3 Reuse Candidates from the task description, explaining how each would be applied
assertion_results.evidence: reuse-analysis.md covers all 3 candidates with detailed sections: (1) apply_filter — direct function call for parsing and SQL generation, (2) advisory list.rs — structural template for Query struct and handler delegation, (3) package_license.rs — direct entity import for JOIN and column reference.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections (constraint 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan lists only: package/endpoints/list.rs (modify), package/service/mod.rs (modify), and tests/api/package_license_filter.rs (create) — exactly matching the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections.
